pr.title: fix(cli): require --oss when --local-provider is supplied
pr.body:
## Summary
- Add `codex_utils_cli::validate_local_provider_requires_oss` and reject `--local-provider` without `--oss` in the three CLI entry points that consume `SharedCliOptions` (TUI `run_main`, `codex exec`, `codex debug prompt-input`). Without this guard, `--local-provider ollama --model …` was silently dropped and the session fell back to the default cloud provider.
- Document the supported `codex --oss --local-provider <id> --model <slug>` startup and `codex resume <thread> --oss --local-provider …` resume workflow in `docs/config.md`, and refresh the `--local-provider` help text to state that `--oss` is required.

## Why this shape
- `--oss` stays the mode gate; `--local-provider` is not promoted to a standalone startup mode. This keeps the diff vs. upstream `openai/codex` small and aligns with the per-task routing already on `feat/local-llm-provider-overrides`.
- The validator lives in `codex-utils-cli` so all three call sites share one error message and one set of unit tests.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo test -p codex-utils-cli`
- [x] `cargo test -p codex-cli --test debug_prompt_input`
- [x] `cargo build -p codex-cli -p codex-tui -p codex-exec`
- [x] `cargo clippy -p codex-utils-cli -p codex-tui -p codex-exec -p codex-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- [x] `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- [ ] Manual: `codex --local-provider ollama --model gemma4:…` exits with the new error
- [ ] Manual: `codex --oss --local-provider ollama --model gemma4:…` completes one turn against local Ollama
- [ ] Manual: `codex resume <thread> --oss --local-provider ollama --model gemma4:…` resumes against local Ollama

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
pr.author: ehrvs
